figures.caption: Fig. 1: Predictive-coding-inspired variational recurrent neural network. 2 a. Structure and information processing of Predictive-coding-inspired variational recurrent neural3 network (PV-RNN). PE: prediction error. KLD: Kullback-Leibler divergence. b. Examples of4 prediction and observed data during learning. EEG signals are visualized by selecting 35 channels from the original channels for visual clarity. The red, blue, and green lines represent6 EEG, ECG, and respiratory signals, respectively. Three EEG channels are selected for visual7 clarity. 8

Fig. 4: Multiscale dynamics, directional asymmetry, and diverse time lags of latent2 variables in multimodal-associative layer. 3 a. Multiscale spectral properties of the latent variables related to brain and bodily signals in the4 multimodal-associative layer (z(2)). The power spectral density (PSD) of the 10 variables5 identified as reflecting the BBI. Variables are sorted from top to bottom based on the ratio of6 low-frequency power (<1 Hz). b. Directionality and coupling strength of interaction. The7 relationship between the optimal time lag relative to global brain activity and mutual information8 (MI). The x-axis represents the lag at which MI is maximized. The y-axis indicates the coupling9 strength with the brain, and the color scale indicates the coupling strength with the body (max10 MI with ECG or Respiration). Error bars denote standard error (SE). 11

Fig. 6: Global state latent variables self-organize to reflect attentional condition and2 individual identity. 3 a. Two-dimensional t-SNE embedding of global state layer latent variables. Each point4 corresponds to one sequence and is colored by participants ID. Larger points with black outlines5 indicate the centroid of each participant's cluster. b. The same embedding as in Fig.3a, with6 points colored by attentional state. Larger points with black outlines represent the centroid of7 each attentional condition. c. Silhouette scores computed in the original global state latent8 space for condition and identity, compared with permutation-based null distributions obtained by9 random labels. Vertical dotted lines indicate observed values of Silhouette score. 10

Fig. 5: Interoceptive focus enhances association-layer precision and relates to2 psychological and psychiatric traits. 3 a. Example of a time series of posterior uncertainty in the multimodal-associative layer. b.4 Multimodal-associative layer posterior uncertainty (mean σ of each latent variable) is reduced in5 interoceptive attention compared to exteroceptive attention. b. Correlations between primary6 principal component of precision enhancement ( ) and psychological/ psychiatric7 measures. HBC: heartbeat counting task. RRQ: Rumination-Reflection. Questionnaire. 8

Fig. 3: The effects of the latent variables in each layer on the prediction of each modality. 2 Probability of trained latent variables in each module representing either EEG, ECG, and RESP.3 A simultaneous display of colors means the representation of multiple signals. 4
Fig. 2: Reconstructed multimodal signals and physiological properties. 5
